title: Diabetic Retinopathy Prediction
subtitle: Upload retinal images and get fast DR / No-DR predictions.
tip: Tip: lower the threshold to increase sensitivity.
upload: Choose image(s) or a ZIP folder
about: This tool runs trained convolutional neural networks locally on your computer. It is intended for academic/demonstration use, not clinical diagnosis.
models_loaded: Models are loaded from the `results/` folder by default.
supported_formats: Supported input formats: PNG, JPG, JPEG, ZIP.
class_dr: DR means the model detected signs consistent with diabetic retinopathy.
class_nodr: No DR means the model did not detect those signs strongly enough based on the chosen threshold.
pipeline_steps: 1. Upload image → 2. Preprocess to 224×224 → 3. Run model inference → 4. Apply threshold → 5. Show result and explanation
trouble_missing_model: If you see 'Model file not found', run training or choose a model that exists.
trouble_missing_inputs: If results are empty, check that `inputs/images` and `inputs/labels.csv` exist.
trouble_cpu: The CPU warning about AVX2/FMA is normal and does not break inference.
trouble_gradcam: Grad-CAM is experimental and may be slow on large models.
disclaimer: Research/demo only — not for clinical diagnosis.
no_upload: Upload one or more images to get predictions.
session_empty: No predictions yet in this session.
zip_note: Tip: upload a ZIP file to process a whole folder of images.
quality_disclaimer: Quality scores are general-purpose heuristics and not calibrated for retinal fundus images, which are naturally darker than typical photos.
pdf_truncation_notice: PDF report is limited to the first 10 images and a summary of 12 rows. Use the CSV export for the full set.
uncertainty_confident: Confident — model agrees on this prediction
multiclass_intro: Predict the full DR severity (No DR / Mild / Moderate / Severe / PDR) with a conformal prediction set that statistically guarantees coverage.
multiclass_no_models: No 5-class models found. Train them with `python scripts/train.py --arch resnet50_5class`.
multiclass_no_threshold: No conformal threshold available for this model — run `python -m master.run_multiclass_analysis` to fit one.
multiclass_hint_two: Two-class set — likely between adjacent severities; review recommended.
multiclass_hint_refer: Set spans 3+ severity classes — defer to clinician for review.
